levels._teaches: Tap an arrow and it leaves. Nothing is in anyone's way.
An arrow can be blocked. a2 stands in a1's lane, and a4 stands in a3's, so each pair has an order.
An arrow has a BODY, and the body blocks other arrows. The long arrow a2 lies across a1's lane.

ONLY THE HEAD'S LANE COUNTS. a1 bends: its tail runs along row 1, and a2 sits squarely in front of that tail. a1 is still free, because its HEAD is in row 2 and row 2 is clear. The tail leaves by retracing the head's route. This is the rule people get wrong.
A chain. Exactly ONE arrow can move (a6), and the whole board unwinds from it, one arrow at a time, in order.
